summary:
A standardized framework for identifying maternal deaths can be applied across all socio-economic settings, enabling equitable audits irrespective of resources. This challenges the assumption that effective maternal mortality auditing is limited to wealthy countries due to financial or infrastructural constraints.

First sentence should capture the central mechanism—standardized tools work universally—and directly address equity in application. Second must refute the "rich vs. poor" narrative with clear clinical logic tied to feasibility under any conditions.

Final summary maintains two complete sentences without adding external content or interpretation beyond what was stated.
